quizzes.title: Water Damage Restoration Fundamentals — Knowledge Check

questions.questionText: A supply line to a bathroom vanity has burst, releasing clean mains water that has not contacted contaminated materials. How is this water categorised?
options: Category 1 — clean water
Category 2 — significantly contaminated (grey water)
Category 3 — grossly contaminated (black water)
It cannot be categorised until laboratory testing is complete

questions.questionText: Clean (Category 1) water has been sitting in a warm, closed building for several days. What is the correct professional position on its category?
options: It remains Category 1 permanently because it started clean
It can degrade toward Category 2 or 3 over time and should be reassessed, and when unsure treated as the worse category
It automatically becomes Category 3 after 24 hours in all cases
Category does not change once it has been recorded on the first visit

questions.questionText: In the classes of water intrusion, what does Class 4 describe?
options: The least amount of water, affecting only part of a room
An entire room with carpet and underlay affected and wicking up walls
Specialty drying situations where water is bound in low-permeability materials such as hardwood, plaster or concrete
Any job involving Category 3 water regardless of how much water is present

questions.questionText: Before entering standing water near electrical fittings on an Australian job site, what is the correct approach?
options: Enter immediately because 230 V mains is low enough to be safe in water
Ensure the supply is isolated where needed, use a residual current device (safety switch/RCD) on portable equipment, and use only tested-and-tagged leads
Only wear rubber boots and proceed, as boots fully remove the electrical risk
Switch off one power point and assume the whole area is now de-energised

questions.questionText: You are working with Category 3 (black water / sewage). Which PPE approach is appropriate?
options: Gloves only, since sewage dries quickly
Full skin protection — fluid-resistant coveralls, gloves, boots and eye protection — plus P2 or higher respiratory protection fit-checked to the wearer
A dust mask and ordinary work clothes
No PPE is required once the bulk water has been extracted

questions.questionText: What is the purpose of taking a 'dry standard' reading during moisture assessment?
options: To record the outdoor humidity for the day
To provide a reference reading from unaffected material of the same type, which becomes the target the wet material is dried back to
To calibrate the thermal imaging camera against the moisture meter
To set an arbitrary fixed number that applies to every job regardless of the building

questions.questionText: Why must dehumidification be balanced with airflow during structural drying?
options: Because airflow alone, without removing the evaporated moisture, just raises the humidity in the room and stalls drying
Because dehumidifiers replace the need to measure moisture readings
Because airflow lowers the room temperature below freezing
Because dehumidification is only needed in dry inland climates

questions.questionText: On a standard Australian 10 A GPO circuit (approximately 2300 W total at 230 V, often shared across several outlets), how should drying equipment be powered?
options: Plug in as many machines as physically fit into the available outlets
Add up each machine's actual current draw at 230 V, keep the total safely below each circuit's rating, and spread load across separate circuits
Assume every outlet is on its own dedicated 20 A circuit
Run all equipment from a single power board on one outlet to keep leads tidy

questions.questionText: Visible mould is present in a water-damaged room. What is the correct drying approach?
options: Run air movers at full speed to dry the mould as fast as possible
Do not run air movers; dry using air filtration devices (AFDs/air scrubbers) and dehumidifiers only under containment, and introduce air movers only after remediation and a clearance
Air movers are fine as long as a dehumidifier is also running
Open all windows and use air movers to push the spores outside

questions.questionText: Why does measured drying documentation matter in the Australian insurance context?
options: It is optional paperwork that only slows the job down

Insurers and assessors expect measured evidence — readings trending to a dry standard — not just 'it looks dry', and a clear dated file protects the customer, technician and insurer if a claim is later questioned
It replaces the need to obtain authority for scope changes
It allows the technician to guarantee a fixed dry time to the client
